meta.title: Image Converter Plus — Convert, Resize, and Protect Your Photos
meta.description: Convert images between JPEG, PNG, HEIC, TIFF, and AVIF. Adjust quality, resize, and strip metadata — all on your device.

hero.headline:
Convert. Resize.
Protect.
hero.subtitle: Batch image conversion with privacy controls — entirely on your device.
hero.availability: Available on iPhone and iPad. Requires iOS 16 or later.

formats.headline: Every format you need.
formats.body: Convert between the most popular image formats. Choose the right balance of quality, compatibility, and file size.

features.headline: Precision at every step.
features.body: Fine-tune quality, resize, rename, and preview — all before converting a single image.

cards.title: Quality Control
cards.description: Adjust quality from 40% to 100% for lossy formats. See the estimated file size update in real time.

cards.title: Resize
cards.description: Scale images from 50% to 200%. Set per-image overrides or apply a preset to the entire batch.

cards.title: File Naming
cards.description: Add a prefix or suffix to converted files. Name each image individually or apply a rule to all.

cards.title: Presets
cards.description: 4 built-in presets for one-tap conversion. Customize format, quality, size, and naming in each.

privacy.headline:
Your photos carry more
than you think.
privacy.body: GPS coordinates. Camera model. Timestamps. Image Converter Plus strips hidden metadata before you share — with granular control over what stays and what goes.

workflow.headline: Fits right into your workflow.
workflow.shareBody: Convert images without opening the app. Select photos in any app, tap Share, and convert in one step. Pro users get batch conversion and all formats right from the Share Sheet.
workflow.historyBody: Every conversion is saved with a thumbnail. Re-export to Photos, Files, or the Share Sheet anytime. Set auto-delete to clean up old conversions automatically.

onDevice.headline:
No servers. No uploads.
No tracking.
onDevice.body: Every image is processed locally on your device. There are no cloud services, no analytics, and no ads. An internet connection is only needed to make a purchase.

pro.headline:
Unlock everything.
Once.
pro.body: A single purchase. No subscription. No recurring charges. Shared with your family through Family Sharing.

features.title: TIFF & AVIF
features.description: Unlock all five output formats for every use case.

features.title: Metadata Stripping
features.description: Full EXIF and metadata removal with three privacy presets.

features.title: Batch Share Extension
features.description: Convert multiple images at once directly from the Share Sheet.

accessibility.headline: Designed for everyone.
accessibility.body: Built with accessibility from the start. Available in 60 languages with an in-app language picker.

meta.title: Privacy Policy - Image Converter Plus
meta.description: Privacy Policy for Image Converter Plus. All image processing happens on your device — nothing is uploaded, tracked, or shared.

hero.title: Privacy Policy
hero.subtitle: Your photos stay yours. Everything is processed on your device — nothing is uploaded, tracked, or shared.

cards.heading: On Your Device
paragraphs: All your images, conversion history, and preferences are stored only on this device. Nothing ever leaves without your explicit action.
bullets: Imported and converted images in app sandbox storage
Your format, quality, and resize preferences
Temporary files in /var/tmp/ — automatically cleaned on each launch
Keychain storage — only a hash of redeemed promo codes, never the code itself
App Group container — shares only Pro purchase status with the Share Extension

cards.heading: What We Don’t Collect
paragraphs: Image Converter Plus doesn’t collect any personal information. We don’t know who you are, what you convert, or how you use the app.

cards.heading: Photos & Images
paragraphs: When you select photos, Image Converter Plus only sees the specific images you choose — never your entire library. The app uses limited photo library access and stores only localIdentifiers for selected assets.
If a selected photo is stored in iCloud, the app temporarily downloads it to your device with a 2-minute timeout. These ephemeral copies are cleaned up automatically on launch.
All image processing happens locally using your device’s hardware. Converted images are saved to your device and shared only when you choose to share them.
You can remove metadata (EXIF, GPS, camera info, timestamps, author details, device identifiers, edit history, and embedded thumbnails) from converted images to protect your privacy when sharing.

cards.heading: Purchases
paragraphs: Purchases are handled entirely by Apple through StoreKit 2. We never see your payment information, Apple ID, or billing details.
Your purchase status is verified locally using Apple’s cryptographic transaction verification. No server calls are made to validate purchases.
Promo codes are validated locally using HMAC — only a cryptographic hash is stored in the Keychain, never the code itself.

cards.heading: Share Extension
paragraphs: The Share Extension runs as an isolated process separate from the main app. It converts images using your default preset directly from the system Share Sheet.

Temporary files created by the extension are cleaned up after use. The extension does not persist conversion history. The only data shared between the main app and the extension via App Group is your Pro purchase status.

cards.heading: Data Retention
paragraphs: You’re in control of your data. You can delete your conversion history anytime from the History tab. Automatic cleanup is available in Settings — choose 7 days, 30 days, 90 days, or keep conversions forever.
On each launch, the app cleans up orphaned files (imports, outputs, and thumbnails not linked to any history record) and removes temporary directories.

Deleting the app removes all associated data — images, history, preferences, and App Group storage. The only exception is a small Keychain entry (promo code hash), which persists across installs as is standard for iOS Keychain items.

cards.heading: Third-Party Services
paragraphs: Image Converter Plus uses only Apple’s built-in frameworks. There are no third-party analytics, advertising SDKs, or external tracking tools.
The app works completely offline. An internet connection is only needed when making a purchase.

cards.heading: Privacy Manifest
paragraphs: The app includes an Apple Privacy Manifest declaring NSPrivacyTracking: false. No data types are collected or tracked, and no tracking domains are contacted.

cards.heading: Contact
paragraphs: Questions about privacy? Reach out anytime at imageconverterplus.support@gmail.com

meta.title: Terms of Use - Image Converter Plus
meta.description: Terms of Use for Image Converter Plus. Read the license, purchase terms, and usage guidelines for the app.

cards.heading: Agreement
paragraphs: By downloading or using Image Converter Plus, you agree to these terms. If you don’t agree, please don’t use the app.

cards.heading: License
paragraphs: Image Converter Plus grants you a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use the app for personal, non-commercial purposes on devices you own or control.
This license extends to the Share Extension (“Convert with Image Converter Plus”), which is part of the app. The app requires iOS 16.0 or later.

cards.heading: Your Content
paragraphs: You retain all rights to the images you convert using Image Converter Plus. The app doesn’t claim any ownership of your content.
You’re responsible for ensuring you have the right to use and convert any images you import into the app.

cards.heading: Free & Pro Tiers
paragraphs: Image Converter Plus is free to download and use. The free tier includes conversion between JPEG, PNG, and HEIC formats, one image at a time.
Pro is a one-time purchase (not a subscription) that unlocks:
Pro is shared with your family through Family Sharing.
Promo codes grant the same access as a Pro purchase. Each promo code can be redeemed once per device.
bullets: Batch conversion in the Share Extension

cards.heading: Purchases
paragraphs: All purchases are processed by Apple through StoreKit 2 and are subject to Apple’s terms and conditions.
The Pro upgrade is a one-time non-consumable purchase — not a subscription. You pay once and keep it forever.
Tips are optional consumable purchases that support development. They do not unlock any additional features.
All sales are final. Refund requests should be directed to Apple. You can restore your purchases anytime from Settings.

cards.heading: Acceptable Use
paragraphs: You agree not to:
bullets: Reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble the app
Use the app for any illegal purpose
Attempt to bypass any limitations or restrictions

cards.heading: Disclaimer
paragraphs: Image Converter Plus is provided “as is” without warranties of any kind. We don’t guarantee the app will be error-free or uninterrupted.
We’re not responsible for any loss of data or images. Please keep backups of important files.

cards.heading: Limitation of Liability
paragraphs: To the maximum extent permitted by law, the developer shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages arising from your use of the app.

cards.heading: Changes
paragraphs: We may update these terms from time to time. Continued use of the app after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.

cards.heading: Contact
paragraphs: Questions? Reach out at imageconverterplus.support@gmail.com

meta.title: Support - Image Converter Plus
meta.description: Get help with Image Converter Plus. Find answers to common questions about converting, resizing, and protecting your photos.

hero.title: Support
hero.subtitle: Find answers to common questions about converting, resizing, and protecting your photos.

formats.label: Formats
formats.title: Five formats, one app.
formats.description: Convert between the most popular image formats with full quality control.

items.question: How do I convert images?
items.answer: Tap the Photos or Files button to import images. Choose your output format and quality, then tap Convert. Converted images appear in the History tab, where you can save or share them.

items.question: Can I convert multiple images at once?
items.answer: Yes. Select as many images as you need and convert them all in a single batch. Each image can have its own format, quality, size, and file name settings.

items.question: Can I import from iCloud Drive or other apps?
items.answer: Yes. Tap the Files button to browse iCloud Drive, Dropbox, Google Drive, or any file provider on your device. You can also use the Share Extension to convert images directly from other apps.

items.question: What does the quality slider do?
items.answer: The quality slider controls compression for JPEG, HEIC, and AVIF formats. Higher values produce better quality at larger file sizes. PNG and TIFF are always lossless and ignore this setting.

items.question: How do I resize images?
items.answer: Use the size setting to scale images from 50% to 200% of the original dimensions. You can set a default for all images or customize each one individually.

items.question: Can I rename files during conversion?
items.answer: Yes. You can add a prefix or suffix to all converted files using a naming rule, or rename each image individually before converting.

items.question: What are presets?
items.answer: Presets save a combination of format, quality, size, and naming settings for one-tap conversion. The app includes 4 built-in presets, and you can reorder or hide them in Settings.

items.question: Can I remove metadata from images?
items.answer: Yes. Image Converter Plus can strip EXIF data including GPS location, camera information, timestamps, author details, device identifiers, edit history, and embedded thumbnails. Choose from preset privacy modes or customize exactly what to remove.

items.question: Are my images uploaded anywhere?
items.answer: No. All processing happens entirely on your device. There are no servers, no uploads, no analytics, and no ads. The app works completely offline — an internet connection is only needed to make a purchase.

items.question: Where are my converted images saved?
items.answer: Converted images are saved in the app’s History tab. From there, you can save them to your Photo Library, export to Files, or share via AirDrop, Messages, Mail, and more.

items.question: How does the Share Extension work?
items.answer: Select images in any app, tap the Share button, and choose Image Converter Plus. Your images are converted using your default preset without opening the main app. Pro users can convert multiple images at once from the Share Sheet.

items.question: Can I auto-delete old conversions?
items.answer: Yes. Go to Settings and choose an auto-delete period — 7 days, 30 days, 90 days, or never. Old conversions are removed automatically to save storage.

items.question: What’s included in the Pro upgrade?
items.answer: Pro is a one-time purchase that unlocks TIFF and AVIF formats, full metadata and privacy controls, per-image setting overrides, and batch conversion in the Share Extension. There is no subscription — and it’s shared with your family through Family Sharing.

items.question: How do I restore my purchase?
items.answer: Go to Settings and tap Restore Purchases. Make sure you’re signed in with the same Apple Account you used for the original purchase. If you’re part of a Family Sharing group, the purchase is shared automatically.

items.question: The app isn’t working properly.
items.answer: Try closing and reopening the app. If the issue persists, restart your device and make sure you’re running the latest version from the App Store. Still having trouble? Contact us with details about what’s happening and we’ll help.

items.question: A conversion failed. What should I do?
items.answer: Make sure your device has enough free storage. Very large images may require more available space. Try converting fewer images at once, or reduce the output size. If the problem continues, contact us with the image format and size.

items.question: My Pro purchase isn’t showing up.
items.answer: Go to Settings and tap Restore Purchases. Make sure you’re connected to the internet and signed in with the correct Apple Account. If you purchased through Family Sharing, ensure that sharing is still active in your family group.

requirements.heading: System Requirements
items: Works entirely offline — no internet connection required
